We did really well in spite of ourselves, not because of ourselves, but knowing that information moving forward in that particular case, what we're simply going to do is we're just going to make the offer itself a, a one payment instead of having continuity and then positioning the two things at the end of it.
And then we're just going to make a, make a way for them to pay for the continuity on the backend after they see success, not before they see success.
And we'll probably double or triple our profits.

So you're going to take a before picture and an after picture and the winner's going to win $100,000.
Right.
And you get a t-shirt that says I lost six inches or whatever.
But now you transition from this like boring, like it's a tape measure to like an offer.
And I can't, um, in fact, we do these, these events here in our office called fat events, sensor funnel hackathon and Steven Larson, who, um, he teaches half of it.
And, uh, the first thing he doesn't get up there and teaches a section on creative offer, he has them raise their hands, stand up, raise your hand.
Everyone repeat after me.
I will never sell a product again.
We'll never sell a product again.
We do not sell products.
Products are commodities.
As soon as you have a commodity, it's a race to the bottom.
Cause the dude, you know, one dude in China gets this for a dollar, I guess for 70 cents and everyone's racing to the bottom.
When you transition it from a, from a product to an offer, I'm taking this thing, I'm, I'm decommodatizing it by making an offer.
Now I can sell this thing for $200 worth every now selling for $1.99.
Right.
Because now it's an offer and they're getting more value based on it.
And people, and this is my, my anti-Amazon slash Google search thing, but it's just like when someone, when someone is searching for a solution, they are price shopping and it's going to, they're going to go to the bottom possible thing, right?
That's why I don't like, I don't, there's not a lot of leverage there.
Search engines.
I, I focus on interruption marketing.
I want to Facebook ad right pop out in the middle of it.
And I'm going to say, Hey, and, uh, and they'll sell people on the perceived value of the thing as opposed to the commoditization of the thing.
Right.
The perceived value is where I can actually sell things are actually worth to me.
Right.
And that's what webinars give the ability to do is you're selling the perceived value of the thing.
You're not just selling the thing.
Cause in all honesty, like we have a website builder.
That's what click funnels is.
And I got some, it doesn't be teaching people how we do stuff.

I mean, obviously the offer's got to be incredible and amazing, but especially today, there's more opportunity and options than ever before.

When you do the ClickFunnels Auto webinar, how do you do this in an auto webinar format?
How do you do scarcity there?
We basically say for those who get started, we give them an unlimited count.
So get unlimited funnels, unlimited band with stuff like that.
That's the thing that we, that's again a hard thing to do.
So we basically, we usually say something for the first 50 or anyone who signs up before this, I think auto webinars, because there's like three days after the webinar hits until it goes offline.
So it's like, and we get started before we close cart.
So most urgency scarcity happens like in the last email sequence, which is the last days where most of sales come in or half the sales come in.
But one thing we started recently that's worked the best because it actually has real urgency and scarcity is we have, and I'm in a position in a way different now that makes your $150 coaching session I did, but we have a couple of coaches we call them ClickStart coaches and the people do pay, I don't know, five grand for like eight calls helping to get onboard in the ClickFunnels.
And so what we do now is we give someone an on-boarding call with the ClickStart coach.
And we, I think we have two or maybe three people now that do that.
And so it's like, look at the size of this room or look at, you know, right now on the webinar we've got 800 people on, you know, my guess is from that we're gonna have 150 guys sign up and there's three of them and they have calendars and there's first come first served.
So like if you go fast, you log in the members area, there's a button, you go schedule your car.
Like you can have a call tonight, you can have a call tomorrow, you can have it in two or three days.
But if you wait, the longer you wait, the longer you get pushed down.
You may not have your QuickStart call for a week, two months, three months, depending on how fast you're getting.
And so it's first come first served basis.
And that's been the best thing for us now because it's not fake scarcity, it's actually legitimate.
It is real, there's a real calendar, they really have to schedule and people understand that.
Anyone who's like excited when we start now, like they want to rush and get queued up as quick as possible.
So that's been the best thing for us recently is having those $150 coaching sessions queued up.
Yeah, that's interesting.
And I like the fact to a software, because we play with this is waving the cap of usage if they buy before a certain period, right?
That has more leverage than just about anything.
Yeah, let me hear about that.
So when I start into my stack, the first thing, when I got who here wants ClickFunnels for free for the next year, oh, I never was going crazy.

So I introduce the restraint so I can take it off later.
And that's been such a huge thing for the close race.

Now that's great.
Because when we saw software for the first year and a half we were in business, we found the exact same thing as the case.
In fact, like, because we didn't have this, you have such a good solution.
We have these very tactical software.
They do these one thing and they do it well.
So we would never offer unlimited.
Nobody could ever get unlimited anything.
It was always, you could use this for 100 websites or you could use this for 100 clients.
And then what I would do on the webinar is, I would double that and then I double it again.
And now we would say, listen, normally 100 clients cost you $9.97 for the software, right?
I'm gonna give you 400 clients and I'm gonna give it to you for $4.97.
So we'd have the price of the offer and we quadruple the value so they would do the math.
This is a $4,000 offer that I'm getting for $500.
But they could actually add it up because you can't put a number on unlimited.
Now I wouldn't do that in that case with ClickFunnels because unlimited is the key because it's a one size fits all type of solution.
Like when we were doing these very narrowly defined types of software, we were selling things that the rest of the market would sell for $10.
You know, all those guys launching these like ClickMang products and these JV Zoo products and these whatever products that were out there, right?
Special Warrior, special offer products, they would sell this out for for $10, right?
We would come in, we would acquire their business.
We would acquire the rights to sell it, not even their business.
We would just say, hey, listen, I'll pay you three grand for the software.
They'd be like, three grand?
I spent a lot more of that.
I'm like, yeah, but you sell it for $10, right?
And you're launching over, we have no more way to sell this thing.
Yeah, I'm essentially giving you 300 customers today.
What was the last time you got 300 customers for a 10 second deal, right?
Oh, never, okay, well, all right, here's $3,000.
And we take that, we'd go over there and we'd rebuild the software, because it was always broken by the way, because nobody can sell and support software for 10 or 20 or $30, right?
But what we would also do is we'd develop a webinar pitch for it.
We would add information to it like we were talking before because that usually didn't exist.
And then we would make the software better and we had good support, so we would sell the support behind it.
But essentially, we would take the same software that was selling for $10 over there and we would sell it for $297 on a webinar.
We changed the method in how it was sold because nobody was using webinars.
We would change the delivery of it and we would change the ability of how we would position it.
And these are the same audience, they wouldn't pay 10 bucks for this stupid software before.
Now they're happy to pay 297 for it and they're making 10 grand from it before they would make nothing for $10.
And I always thought that was fascinating because here's what inevitably happened, all these guys got pissed off at us because I was making more money from their software than they were making.
So here was my solution and I said, why don't you pay me for coaching then?
And why don't you learn how to do what I do instead of get upset that I could do it better than you?
But that is fine, right?
They don't want to hear that.
So it's like, but the thing is with that scarcity, again, you create a constraint intentionally.
So that way you can remove that constraint or you can manipulate that constraint.
So the whole goal of this is for people to understand the value behind it.
It's like, it's not that we're making up value, the value is there, we're uncovering all the value that's buried down that people are having trouble seeing and then shining a light on it so they can see it.
So I love the fact with your thing, Russell, it's like, you don't skimp either.
Your software that you offer to them for free is the $297 a month version, right?
So do the math, 300 times six is $1800.
You get that for free when you buy a $997 product or whatever the price point of it is now, right?
I remember when I was promoted, it was $997 for six months.
Yeah.
So here's what's great about that.
You were talking about the long-term funnel impact of this from my understanding is now it's, somebody's less likely to cancel because it's not a yes or no.
It's do I want to stay at this level or do I want to step it down?
So now if they step down to a $197 or a $97 level, whatever the levels are, now it's a good deal for them too because they can gauge their usage.
But if they're kicking butt and they're using it at this level, they're not going to want to step down.
So you've engineered a situation from my understanding that they can see the value, it's built into it.
It's less for free than if they paid for it on its own.
And by the time they get around to paying for it, that's the tail of the offer to make that revenue, you're going to make more money from them because the concept behind it is there's service ahead of the sales.
But the scarcity is powerful.
And in your case, you're using scarcity if I understand it correctly in terms of availability.
You're going to get this thing.
I'm not taking it away from it.
But do you want to wait forever or do you want to get it right away?
And that's reason why.

You know, in that case, I didn't really, I honestly did not care, you know?
At that point I was too tired to really care.
But the powerful thing about that scarcity, the reason why was you're going to buy, you can buy today and pay less or you can wait till tomorrow and pay more.
I don't care.
But the reason I'm giving this to you today is because it's a better deal than if I sold it to you tomorrow.
And that's the simplest reason why.
So the reason why is, you know, the one I'm sure you've heard this, right?
Is a, you know, we find that people that act immediately on a good idea tend to be more successful.
And so therefore to encourage you and to lock in this habit of taking action immediately, I'm going to reward you for immediate action in the following ways.
And then that's your reason why for your scarcity.
To introduce a habit.
But it's just, it could be any reason why.
I'm offering you the scarcity because my mom told me to.
Okay, got it.
Now it makes sense.
But so many people do scarcity because they learn it in, you know, marketing 101 textbook.
You need scarcity so they just do it, but it falls flat because they don't connect to reason why.
And that's why I like yours is, hey, I want to help everybody.
But, you know, there's a line.
The reason why is I have limited resources in which I can help people.
So the sooner you start, the sooner I can help you.
And that's really powerful.
And removing the limitation on the webinar, I think if I remember correctly, the reason why essentially is on that is, you're awesome.
You're here with me today.
And I really like you because you're here today.
So therefore I'm gonna do you a favor.
Because you guys are awesome.
And because I love Jason.
And because I hear Grant Cardone's event, because we're at Mandolin Bay.
Because, yeah, just because something cool happened today, you're getting this awesome.
Yep.
Yeah, it doesn't have to be that powerful.
Now, if it is powerful, if it's a strong reason why that is legitimate, it tends to be more effective.
So like in the case that you were describing, is that a so, that is inarguable.
There is no doubt that that's true.
And nobody could dispute that.
And so like, if you can create scarcity, where that's the case, that's why damaged goods sales are so powerful.
We got 75 of these things.
They normally sell for five grand, but because there's a tiny little scratch on the bottom left-hand corner, it's not in new condition anymore.
I only have 75 of them though in my warehouse.
And I gotta get rid of them.
So therefore you can get it for $1,500.
But when they're gone, they're gone.
It's like, that is so, here's the best scarcity I ever did.
Purely by accident.
And I'm too scared to try to do this on purpose because it feels too sneaky, but just to really understand the power of this.
Way back in the day, before I had like staff and stuff, I still had to do most of the stuff myself.
I set up an order form for this webinar I was doing for this affiliate, it was like 2008.
And it was supposed to be three payment options of $97 a piece.
Because back then I was testing this offer.
I've tested everything.
I've tested full pay only with no option for payment plan.
I've tested payment plan only with no option for full pay.
And I've tested A and B, either pay full or installments.
And I could talk about which one I prefer and which one I normally do here in a second.
But at this particular time in my career, I eliminated the full pay.
I only allowed them to pay in installments.
It was a stupid idea by the way.
But whatever, I was testing it out.
So in this case, the offer was, when you get started today, you can get started with just 97 right now and then two more installments of the same of 97 space 30 days apart.
It wasn't as clever as your microcontinuity.
Or was it microcontinuity?
Is that what it's called, right?
Yeah.
Yeah, so many people knocked that off that I didn't remember because there is all these different versions of it.
But you were the first.
When you turn continuity from an open ended to a fixed session, the conversion rates can be high.
Like we weren't smart enough to see it back then, but essentially by saying, you can get started for just $97 today and two more installments in 97, it made the offer sound softer, the price sound good.
For people that have trouble selling higher ticket experiment with that, because you might find that makes you more money until you get better, right?
But in this particular case, I set up the order form, but instead of making it three installments, I accidentally make it two.
So I went on the webinar and I hard sold this.
I was like, all right, it's three installments.
You're gonna pay three times.
And then the first five people that ordered like Jason, I don't know if I got lucky or you screwed up or what.
It's only two times on the order form.
And I'm like, oh no.
I was like, I could not fake that because it was a complete accident.
I was like, I apologize to the fillers.
I say, here's what's really sucks because this guy on here with me, he gets 50% of the sale and I've essentially lost him a bunch of money.
So I'm okay with eating whatever I have to do and still paying him what I told him I was gonna pay him.
And he's like, no, that's okay.
I'll share it with you.
I said, but here's what I'm gonna do.
I'm gonna honor my mistake.
So anybody that's on this webinar lies.
Once this webinar ends, the very first thing I'm doing is logging in there and changing that and updating that order form.
But if you buy before the webinar ends, I will honor my mistake to you and you get it for just two installments instead of three.
At that time was the best close right ever.
Because the scarcity was so real because it was screwed up.
But I've ever since then, Russell, I've always wanted to do that intentionally.
I just never had the nerve to be like, oops, my mistake, that was supposed to be five installments, but it's only one.
I broke it, but after this webinar, I'll fix it or something.
I saw Dave Lindemob is something kind of similar to that, where he had his order form for a thousand dollar course.
And then there was like an order form bump for a $5,000 thing.
But then what he did is like, he's like showing people, or like, hey I'm gonna show you, he's gonna order for my here.
He's like, okay, I'm gonna do something really, really cool.
This is our $5,000 upsell.
It's amazing, it's so good.
When we're here now, if you guys buy live on this webinar, don't check that check mark, just fill it out.
But I will go in back in tonight and unlock that $5,000 training to all of your accounts when you start right now.
And all of a sudden people are like, oh my gosh, because it's like they want that thing, but it was just like, you get it for free.
They just saw it, like they could have actually bought it or they get it for free and he killed it with that.
Which is another cool.
That's a brilliant way to position it, because again, establish value.
They know this thing is $5,000.
They know that for them, it's essentially they're gonna get for $1,000, what should be $5,000, but they have to do it in a limited amount of time.
And then the reason why is just simply because you're here right now and I like you, and let's get started.
That's a really powerful thing.
And so Jason asked me, you know, the scarcity compounder.
So the thing is people think you can only do one type of scarcity one time on a webinar.
And we typically like to stack scarcity.
So I've found there's essentially, there's not that many levers of scarcity.
There's like the price either goes up, you know, or a bonus goes away, or the offer goes away and you can't get it anymore, right?
Or you have to like, you only get a portion of the thing in the future as opposed to the whole thing.
I think that's like the only four types of scarcity that exist.
I mean, actually like, I don't know if that's all of them.
I can't remember.
I actually documented them all at some point in time, right?
But essentially the price either goes up or you can't buy the offer at all.
The access goes away.
A bonus goes away or a portion of the offer goes away.
That's it.
So when you think that's it, that's all the scarcity.
Everything else comes with the creative deployment of that scarcity, the positioning of it, the payoff and the believability.
And so we like to do all of those things.
So we essentially, when we offer this thing, we'll say, listen, the price is this now.
It goes up to this at this date.
And then after that, it goes away and without any idea of ever coming back again.
You know, for example, right?
So that's two levels of scarcity.
You get penalized once for waiting in terms of a fee.
You get penalized the second time by not even getting access at any price, right?
And then we say on top of that, these bonuses are only gonna be available shipped with this course for free for a limited time because our plan is to sell them over here later on and do X, Y and Z.
Now, obviously you can't lie.
That has to be true, right?
The good thing about bonuses is they're easy to create.
I can rotate bonuses in and out at will, you know?
And I can create bonuses that anybody who's already bought gets all future bonuses for free, but anybody who buys in the future doesn't necessarily get the past bonuses.
So we reward the people that act early.
And so that's the other level of scarcity involved now is you get exclusive bonuses that will never be given again.
Think of those collector's editions.
You know, that's the idea I always try to decap.
Remember those quarters?
You get a quarter for each state, you put it in there, right?
Yep.
They're now selling quarters.
They're selling quarters for 50 cents.
That's a sales pitch, right?
I mean, I don't know what they're selling it for, but they're selling a quarter for more than a quarter because you're collecting it because it's a scarce resource.
I don't recommend you sell quarters for 50 cents.
Don't get me wrong, right?
But the idea here is we could create a bonus and then throw that bonus away and next we create another exclusive bonus.
Now, the earlier you buy it, whether it's just a customer, the more bonuses you get.
You know, like so we did this thing for Bob Proctor's audience and we created a bonus.
I'm literally doing it later tonight because I'm so insane Russell, one three hour training wasn't enough for me today.
I had to do two of them, you know?
It just landed that way.
There's no other way around it.
But I created this bonus intentionally because I knew his audience of consultants that we were speaking for really wanted to know how to do webinars as a consultant.
So here's what I did.
I said, if you buy before this time, you will get access to this bonus 100% for free and it's a live training.
So that's reason why scarcity is why you gotta act now.
Cause you can't come to it live if you sign up the day after we did it.
So that's powerful.
That's an event based scarcity.
And then the second thing is, is I molded that bonus specifically to their objection.
Then we emailed the whole list of genius webinar customers and say, come on live and do this training with us.
So now any customer who's a bonus of our customers in the future can come on.
I mean, in the past, who's a customer gets this for free and they didn't even expect to.
So existing customers get excited which makes them more likely to buy other stuff from us.
New customers are more likely to say yes.
Now next month when we roll that out to his whole list, not just the consultants, I can make the decision at that time Russell, if we pull that bonus out completely from the product and never let anybody ever again get it.
I can literally say, we will never ship this with this product ever again.
I could either sell it publicly at that point if I wanted to, or even better if this is the hard line approach.
I will never sell it again.
I have signed a contract.
Here it is that forbids me from ever selling this bonus again or distributing it outside of these terms.
And now people have to buy it because there's no way they'll ever see it ever again.
Now that one takes guts and that one has to be time at the right situation and the right time.
And I would give it to every existing customer at that point and I would position us, here's the good thing about being my customer.
I keep giving you stuff even after you expect me not to.
And this is an exclusive thing since you're a customer, nobody else in the future at this point will ever get it again.
So by the way, if you wanna refer a friend right now, why don't you?
So I'm always selling ABC.
But what's even better about this is, I mean, I don't know about you Russell, I could create a thousand different bonuses.
I never run out of ideas to value add.
I never run out of new things.
And every day there's new technology being developed, the new concepts and new interesting things.
I mean, I could teach people how to sell Bitcoin on webinars and I couldn't do that five years ago.
It's like, there's a million new opportunities.
We can never run out of bonuses.
So it's okay to not be worried that I'll never be able to distribute this bonus again.
And we've done that before.
And that's the collector's edition.
We will never circulate this currency ever again.
And for the right audience at the right time, that's incredibly powerful.
Now, in most cases, we don't compound scarcity like that, but that's every so often, it makes sense to pull the big dogs out.
But you wanna stack multiple scarcity price goes up, offer goes away, bonus goes away, all those on their own are powerful, put them all together.
And now people just, they're just short circuiting and their ability to just- Cause I know with my audience, a lot of people struggle.
Like they spend a year trying to develop just the product.
Yep.
And then now you're saying all these bonuses and I'm sure someone are like, I can't even think of one thing.
How do you create bonus?
Like I look for two things, number one, just in a short format, but like how do you come up with the idea of some bonuses and how do you create them quickly?
Cause I understand I do the same thing, but most people miss this.
They spend years trying to develop the product.
And it's like, there's faster ways to do this.
And like, and in fact, I had one minor circle members of their day message me and he was like planning to create his product.
He was gonna get a jet and fly around the country and interview all these people and all sorts of stuff.
And it was gonna be, the cost was like 80 or $90,000 to do this thing.
And I was like- That's ridiculous.
You could get a $100 account to zoom and just record the same interview because people care about if you're in their house or if they just want the information and you save me $80,000 that day.
So I would love for you to kind of explain like how you do it faster.
First of all, this is, I'm glad you're with us because this is a lesson I learned is how do we give that $80,000 to our customers?
I can literally say to my customers, create this for me and the best 15 products that are created, I'm gonna give $10,000 each to them or $5,000 each to them.
And now you just paid 80 grand to create a million dollars worth of value, right?
So the idea of this is called financial engineering.
How do we pay with less money over here to get something that's worth more money over there?
So that's an aside that I just popped in my head.
So ideas for bonuses, first of all, is essentially what you wanna do is, the concept behind it is we wanna create an offer that's unnatural.
Nobody ever could compare with our offer.
Nobody could beat it, nobody could attempt to and they won't even try.
And so that's the philosophy behind that.

But I said, don't focus on what you can sell people.
I said, focus on how you can serve a specific audience.
And then once you serve them by selling them one little thing, wait until they demand other things from you and then sell them those things.
I said, it's one thing to come up with an idea and try to find an audience for it.
But if you have an existing audience because you got a little foot hold in a new market and they're begging you for everything else.
I said, so here's the extra level.
I said, here's what you should do on your website.
Here are the 15 products I'm going to create.
Right now, I think I'm gonna get the biggest impact creating this one product.
But no, sometime in the future these other 14 products are gonna exist.
And then after somebody buys that first product, I said, ask them out of every other product they could create now, which one would you want the most?
And now you're just giving people what they are begging for as opposed to trying to figure it out, right?
Now here's the transition into what I'm talking about here with everybody here.
If you looked at all your competitors who sell the same thing, you sell a similar thing in substance and nature, right?
I mean, ClickFunnels has 150 competitors and they all say that they do it better and they're cheaper as if such a thing ever existed, right?
Yeah, they're like, we're 10 times better and we're 100 times cheaper and we've only made $1,000 online.
Yeah, something doesn't jive right there, right?
But whether it's true or not doesn't matter because it's pollution and the noise of our audience that we have to consider.
And so webinars are the same thing.
It's like, if I can have everything in your offer encompassed in my offer as a small portion of it, I win.
Because I give everything you have and I have a whole bunch more.
Now, if I can do that one time with one competitor, that's great, if I can do all my competitors, then nobody competes anymore.
And so the first thing that everybody has to do is this.
Look at who else is selling similar things to you.
Yes, they're not as good as your thing.
You always have the best product, yada, yada, yada, I hear it every day, right?
But perception rules the marketplace.
Who else is selling and talking to the audience you wanna serve and what are they offering them?
How can we offer everything they offer plus everything we offer?
So literally, we have everything they have and we have all the stuff that they don't have.
And then what you do and you have to do this, it's important, you write down every possible thing that could be, everything.
Don't limit yourself.
A hundred different things you could do, a thousand different things you could do till your hand falls off practically, you write down these offers.
And then you say, if I had to do any one of these, what would be the one I'm excited to do first?
Not the one I think people want the most.
What's the one that excites me the most?
Okay, you circle that one.
And then you say, if I only had 48 hours to create this, how would I do it in a way that somebody, the first time that they go through this could get a benefit from it?
So in one consumption and 48 hours of creation, how could I have a bonus as done?
That's that, you know, it's, you ever read the book, The Goal?
The goal?
The goal.
Oh my God, it's such a great book.
Oh my God, that book.
I want to run right in there, the closet, grab it.
It's called The Theory of Constraints.
There's a section in that, Theory of Constraints.
And this is what this is.
We've created a constraint.
It's actually, you open up more possibility by limiting the possibilities.
It's very paradoxical, right?
So Theory of Constraints is where I learned this from.
So it constrains you in the ability of what you can focus on, the time you can complete it in, and on top of all of that, the outcome.
The outcome is they can't read it 50 times.
They have to read it once and get a benefit from it, okay?
That's how you create a bonus.
And that's how we did it for years, Russell, right?

I mean, you know, because we call it, I call it downstream marketing.
Most people are swimming upstream in their marketing.
They're fighting the tide.
And essentially, I said, convincing a user that they should watch you instead of Ty Lopez, which is what a lot of rookie beginner marketers do.
You say, here's me watching Ty Lopez.
Watch me watch Ty Lopez, essentially, right?
And now you have rapport because you share something with them, but then you can engage them, get their attention.
And then from that, you can leverage that to then transition over to what you want to say.
You know, it's like, you know, the other thing too is I like to spend money.
And I always like to spend money, Russell, even when I didn't have much to spend, is for most audiences, okay, in the marketing world, all of us make up these fictitious prices of what we charge hourly rates for that we never actually charge us for one time back in 1987, right?
And so if you go to a marketing guy and says, listen, I want to interview you and I want to harvest that material and distribute it over here, what's your hourly rate?
You're going to pay through the notes.
But in most of these audiences, like, you know, dog training secrets, right?
You know, I could throw a rock and hit 10 dog trainers here who I could pay less than $100 an hour for.
And I could create unique content created for me just by interviewing them.
The content is not what's important.
This is where everybody screws it up.
It's what type of content will create the most desire in my audience to buy my product because it tips the offer over into, oh my God, this is insane territory, right?
So here's a huge, ooh, this is good, Russell.
I hunt for proof elements.
I don't hunt for products.
I hunt for proof elements.
So let me explain this.
The number one thing that got me excited to do business with Trey Llewellyn was the following.
He had sold more physical products through ClickFunnels than anybody else in the entirety of ClickFunnels.
Number one seller, right?
Yep.
I don't care what I sell at that point.
I have a proof element.
Nobody can match.
I mean, I do care, but, and here's the goal.
I can now claim something that nobody could dispute.
You could say you're better than him.
You could say you know more than him.
You could say you're nicer than him.
You could say you'll work harder than him, but you sure as heck can't say you sold more than him through ClickFunnels.
You can't do it.
You'd be a liar, right?
And nobody attempts to argue that point.
And what have I done?
I have created something that is, you can't, you can't assail it.
Now, so we say to ourselves, who has an incredible story of results out there?
Can we pay them for that story?
And then we can make a bonus with the headline, you know how so and so was able to do blank.
I discovered the secrets.
And now people are buying because they're buying the result.
They're not buying the content.
Now the content is important, but what's more important is what that content can do for them and that's what the bonus becomes.
And so I hunt, I see this all the time on Reddit.
Reddit is great because if you, if you're subscribed to these different subreddits, people will tell a story of something amazing that they did.
Like if you're subscribed, here's a great one.
If you want to sell a keto product, go to the subreddit keto.
They have a whole subreddit, they're a keto.
And every other day you'll see somebody posting a picture about how they lost 107 pounds in the last 12 months.
And they have like the best proof elements, like 150 different angles and photos and shots and stuff like that.
If you reach out to that person and said, listen, if I could pay you $500, could I interview you for an hour and then have exclusivity to be able to distribute that to my paying customers over here?
You think they're gonna say no?
I mean, they might, but they're in the, yes, the next guy, right?
Or here's a better idea, Russell.
You spend 15 weeks obsessing over it and trying to write it yourself.
And when it's done, it's probably a hundred times more educational, but you got no proof elements, right?

My wife and I bought her a car a few years ago and I was like, I hate negotiating with these guys.
I hate screwing around with them and I'm busy.
And I don't wanna do it anyway.
So I have her test drive and I have her find it, I have her do all this work and then I come down at the end and I was like, okay, let's buy this car.

This was like five years ago or something like that.
And I'm like, I don't wanna screw around with this stuff.
First of all, I don't get your normal pay stubs, like, you know, these normal people get, right?
And I was like, is there an easier way I could just show you like the idea that we can underwrite this $19,000 vehicle for or something ridiculous like that?
He goes, well, if you log into your bank account and you could show me the balance on that, then it'd be okay.

So I forgot about that.
I just log into show him the balance.

But yeah, I mean, the idea of showing and telling with the offer is this is why I like to pay experts.
Because if I pay experts money, and I can stack that money, then what I pay the experts is worth more than the price of the product.
But here's the beautiful thing, I only had to pay them once, but I could sell the product forever.
So if I pay $2,000 to get a $1,500 product created, two sales and I'm net profit.
But the psychology behind me saying, I have an asset that you can't buy, I negotiated exclusivity on it, and I paid for it for you, nobody on their own could even argue.
I could spend this on my own and spend my time and get it for $2,000, or I could shortcut the process and get it to him for $1,500.
One of the best webinars we ever did, Russell, was this thing called auction webinar.
I went out there and we secured resale rights to 25 different products.
And here was the contract.
The contract says from the beginning of the webinar until the end of the webinar is the only time you have the ability to resell my products.
And I negotiated to sell it at a price that was lower than they'd ever sold it before.
And I said, the reason why I could do this is because they made me swear and legally bound me to, not being able to sell this ever again because these are under actual prices that people have paid real money for, right?
And now my reason why for my scarcity was powerful.
I had a contract.
I legally had to stop the scarcity.
Now, did I do that intentionally so I could have a strong reason why you bet your butt I did, you know?
Of course I did.
But did I also then get the best deal for them they ever could get?
Yes, I did.

Quick things is again, if the webinar is, we talked about like not advertising for a week cause the only people that show up about 40 years.
Yeah, that's right.
That's a big thing.
Number two, there's two trends in webinars.
One is actually three trends.
Sorry, one is a webinar we traditionally talk about where it's live webinar, you promote it, you do it, you have replays, all that kind of stuff.
That's number one.
Number two, auto webinars.
The biggest thing we found in auto webinars after testing a ton of stuff is we used to do like webinars like today, tomorrow or the next day.
And then we used to show it to like top of the hour and then like every 10 minutes, but every right now, every 10 minutes is the highest converting by far auto webinar for us.
Every 15 minutes is less.
The top of the hour drops like it's brilliant.
So every, so right now somewhere, it's an auto webinar.
Have it start 10 minutes after they registered.
That's the highest converting across the board.
Everyone we're watching.
And then the third type of webinar is doing a webinar where you do the same process instead of like doing whole stack and closing pitching.
It's a takeaway sell, which basically is like, hey, this thing's awesome, but like, we don't sell to everyone to make sure a good fit and push into application form and having phone calls happen on the back of it.
That model, the couple is like you're selling a thousand dollar product on the webinar traditionally.
If you push into a phone application and then call them on the phone, you can instantly double the price with zero resistance.
In fact, your conversions will go up.
So you're selling a thousand dollar thing, you sell 2000, but it also gives you ability to do a second tier.
And we're seeing tons of people doing a 2000 and then an eight to $10,000 thing on the phone and just killing it with it.
And so like how much money you make dramatically goes up.
First off, you double how much money you make from every low ticket sell, but then you're also sprinkling in for every one of these, you're selling three of the $10,000 things, which is basically the course with some kind of one on one component or group coaching or something.
And it's really awesome.
And I wish I had time to go deeper into it.
Damn, that's good.
The first is magic.
You're on the phone with a person and basically say, look, people come in at two different levels.
Some come in around the $2,000, someone close to the 10, where do you see yourself fitting?
And people will actually self select and then they tell you which one to sell them and then you just sell them that thing.
Because someone like me, if you offered me like, hey, there's a two and a 10, what do you want?
I'm gonna say the 10.
10.
I don't buy the cheap version.

Now real quickly, just explain why do you think that is?
You know, best example, I have a friend that's in the dating market.
He sends out nine emails a day.
I was like, what?
He's like, this is the deal.
He's like, most of the time my men are on my list for entertainment, but they got a girlfriend, they're fine.
He's like, I wanna make sure that the second that the girlfriend breaks up and they are in pain when they open their phone, I wanna make sure my email's the top one in because they're by the first person's email they see.
He's like, so I'm entertaining, entertaining, entertaining.
As soon as their hearts ripped out of the chest, I need to be the first email so that they buy.
For me, it's the same thing.
Someone sits on the webinar and they didn't buy, they're not in the pain right now, but they heard it and they're thinking about it, but like a month later, two months later, three months later, something happens or something hasn't happened and in the same spot they were before and now they're ready to buy.
Now they're in pain, now they're business dropped or whatever it was and now they're ready and so people aren't ready all the time.
And so this gives the ability, for you to hit it different times when they're ready.
Yeah, and I think too, a lot of this is, the selling doesn't stop when the webinar's over with.
If you do a good enough job, people will go to bed at night thinking about what you said and they'll turn it around in their head and they'll do this over and over again for many months.
And so the next time you come back on and remind them that this opportunity's available again and you have different scarcity this time or you've lifted the previous scarcity, now they're like, yeah, I screwed up last time, but this is my chance to make it good.
And of course, obviously businesses change, people change, et cetera.
The number one reason most people.
Throw one real quick, throw it in there, yeah.
I'm gonna just cancel all my points, this is too cool.
So I read this hypnosis book one time when I was doing webinars and it was talking about like, you can hypnotize people to do things later, like, hey, tonight when you go to bed, you're gonna be in whatever.
So I started my webinar when there's a part like, you got two options.
Number one is don't do anything and just leave this.
The problem with that is that tonight when you go, and I started going hit and not commands, tonight when you go home, what's gonna happen is you're gonna be thinking about funnels, how it's working in your business, you're gonna understand this, and you're gonna realize that if you didn't sign up for now, you missed the biggest opportunity of your life and you're gonna lay in bed and I try to fall asleep, being six years, something like, man, I need this funnel, I need a funnel, I need a funnel, I need a funnel, and it's gonna drive you insane so much so that when you wake up in the morning, you're gonna be calling my office, calling my family, calling my kids, trying to figure out how to get in.
I do not want you to miss that opportunity.
And so I do that and I've had people mess me up with like, Russell, I was sitting in bed all night stressing out because I knew I needed this and I had to get it.
So I woke up in the morning, first thing I did, I found the webinar replay link and I got it.
And so like, you hypnotically command them to actually be sick to the stomach about not signing up and they will, like that night, the hypnotic command will come back in their head and they're gonna be thinking about it and it's amazing.
That's really good, yeah, it's a post-hypnotic suggestion, right?
Normally done in a positive way, like you'll find yourself relaxing, but we twist the knife instead, or, well, it's true too, because again, and this is important, is most of our audiences need the very thing and want the very thing we have for them, but they're scared to get out of their comfort zone.
They're scared to actually be successful and we have to help them understand that this is okay.
It's okay to move forward, it's okay to do stuff that's different that they wanna do.
We have to help them.
And that's one of the ways that we do that in a very powerful, very direct way.
The number one reason most people's webinars fail is we talked about this, the offer sucks.
You know, it's like, that, if you don't make a strong offer on a webinar, it's gonna fail if your webinar is fantastic otherwise.
And that's a big problem that most people have.
How to balance selling and teaching in your webinar.
The idea here is we don't teach just for education's sake.
We teach to create a different identity or to shift a belief pattern.
And then that case, you don't have to think about how much do I teach, how much do I sell.
You say, what do I gotta do to shift this belief pattern in 45 to 60 minutes?
Now, if you can wrap that around what appears to be step by step or insights or secrets that are revealed, that people are gonna say, well, that was educational as well, all the better, but what you're really doing is saying, how do I shift their identity?
How do I shift their belief pattern into this person?
And then once I do that, that's when you begin selling.
And then on top of that, then you begin stacking the closes and the scarcity to get everybody else on board.
Because selling happens in waves.
Russell, you mentioned this when we were doing the pre-call for this, where you know, you sold click funnels to my audience for about two hours.
And then when you were done, I came and sold for another hour on top of you.
And I was doing all these additional closes and Russell was exactly right.
During his webinar, I took notes on everything that he said, every insight I had, every thought that I saw from my audience and I started developing closes right there on the spot.
If I gotta address this, I gotta say this, I gotta do that, I gotta do that.
Cause my philosophy is selling comes in waves, closing comes in waves.
The first people who will buy, they would have bought if you would have just tapped them with a feather and said, do you want this, right?
These are the people that woke up that morning saying, only if I had a solution for blank and then you provide it.
Or these are the people that won the lottery last night and have come into untold fortunes.
And now here you are selling them the thing that they really want that they couldn't afford before, right?
Doesn't take much to sell them.
The second group of people are the people that are looking at this and they're knowing it's a really good idea and they're just double checking it to make sure it makes sense and then they're gonna buy.
And then the further you get out on these waves, the more it becomes the people that are in serious financial pressure right now, or the people that are extremely stressed out in a bad state, or the people that are extremely desperate but they're so freaked out, they don't know what to do.
And we essentially have to address those audiences differently.
And so my philosophy is somebody has sought through this webinar pitch with Russell for two hours and didn't buy yet, but they're still there.
That tells me that they want to buy.
They just haven't heard the right thing that's gonna make them buy yet, right?
Now my goal is how do I do this in a way that's interesting and engaging?
Otherwise, if you come across as callous and pushy, then you're gonna unsell the people that you just sold, which is, oops, and I've done that before.
You know, sometimes the best way to learn a lesson is to screw it up completely and then know not to do that again, right?
But the way to do it is where you really engage them in a deep conversation where you really hit the heart of the matter and then people can tell you care and then they give you permission to sell them.
And so in that case with Russell is, we sell until they're done buying it, but we've educated and created that shift first and then we can close each additional wave on top of that.
When the first wave is done closing, then we shift the focus to the second wave.
When that wave's done closing, then we shift the focus to the third wave.
We do a whole training of that one.
We talked about the bonuses, make sure that the value there, that's totally good.
Limited offers on Evergreen webinars.
Russell discussed on how you did scarcity there.
Why webinars are perfect for the shyest people?
Russell, why are webinars perfect for the shyest people?
I have my idea, but I'd like to hear yours.
I think it's because you don't have to look them in the eye.
You know what I'm saying?
Just scary.
You see people like webinars, like I'm talking to my screen and somebody's just hard because it's like, you have to get the energy.
We were gonna talk about that.
When I do my webinars, I have a standing desk.
I'm standing here, I'm screening, I'm hands are yelling, but nobody can see me and I don't see them.
I'm just talking to nobody.
So it's kind of comforting knowing that or feeling like, there's no one watching me, even though people are hopefully watching you.
So I think that's a big part of it.
Yeah, and what I like about it is typically the people that are introverts are the ones that really focus on, I mean, I'm hardcore introverted.
Nobody believes me when I say that, but it's true.
I'm comfortable in environments where I thrive though.
And so a lot of shy people is once they get out of their shell, then they go crazy, you know?
And so a lot of people, once they get comfortable on a webinar, that's the only place where they feel comfortable anymore.
And they're better than these crazy, whacked out extroverts that people have trouble relating to, which is mostly the audience.
How to sell a webinar, even if you hate selling in our terrible at it.
Again, this is the idea of you stack an offer that's so incredible, essentially selling dollars for dimes, right?
And then the rest of it just becomes an exercise.
The more you do it, the better you get at it.
Nobody's born to sell well on webinars.
I did 100 webinars before I ever thought I was good, but here's the good news.
It's not like I made $0 on my first 100 webinars, right?
At the time that I was horrible at webinars, Russell, I made so much money then, not knowing how bad I was.
I was more excited about the money I made then than the money I make now, even though the money I make now is ridiculously more, because the money I make now doesn't do too much difference in my lifestyle, but the money I made then took me out of this tiny little efficiency apartment and finally put me in a situation where I could actually take five steps without running into something, you know?
I am really upset.
I remember the first ones how, like I remember doing this one tell seminar, my dad was visiting that day, so he was in the room and I did it, and then afterwards I did $5,000 in sales.
I remember he was just like, he's like, that doesn't happen to normal people.
He's like, that's, he was so confused, and I was just like, this is the coolest thing ever.
I do that in an hour.
You know, people want to make a million dollars before they make $10,000, and that's so silly, because it's like, it's harder to make, by the way, it's harder to make $10,000 than it is to make a million, because to go from nothing to something, that's incredible, but to take something and accent it and to accelerate it, that's easy.
So most of you, you only have to be 5% as good as Russell or I are, because the offer to the right audience presented in an educational framework, which is what a webinar is.
A webinar is essentially, I'm gonna provide you with value, and then after I'm done doing that, then I'm gonna ask you to return the favor by investing with me, and that's really powerful, because straight sales pitching, I mean, I do it, and I like it, because it makes money, but I much rather prefer a situation where I can show you what it's like to be my customer, and then ask you to be my customer.
Not only is that more effective, but it's easier, and that's essentially what you're doing, you're leveraging the vehicle that is the webinar to have that happen.
We hit the scarcity compound, or stacking scarcity atop of each other, contractually obligating scarcity, so the reason why is ridiculous.
Approve a psychological stacking technique that supercharges your audience's desire to buy from you.
So I mentioned this when we were talking about framework, is if my goal is to shift your identity, here's how I do it with authority in the introduction, here's how I do it with commitment, here's where I start chipping away at the objections.
People typically wanna handle a price objection once.
I see this all the time, Russell.
If I'm selling high ticket, I'm, there's 150 times that I've minimized price objection before I ever bring up the price.
You know, if I get somebody, you know, here's an example, in an introduction, anybody on any webinar can say this, isn't it wonderful that we can live in a world where there are seven billion people, and there are 93 million miles from a sun that keeps all seven billion of us alive?
Isn't it wonderful?
Isn't it almost, I daresay it's unlimited the potential that's out there.
Did you know at this point in time, right now in the United States, there is over one million people who will make a million dollars this year?
I didn't know that.
It's not a lack that's existing out there.
It's a, how do we get some of that, how do we take advantage of it?
What have I done there?
I just expanded the opportunity, study flat Earth, that's funny.
I just expanded the opportunity and enlarged their perception.
And now when I showed you how many people make a million dollars, and I asked you for a thousand dollars, that's chump change.
That's an example of psychologically stacking.
Cause then later on, when I show an example of how this guy made a million dollars with it, and I'd say, you know what, but he wouldn't pay 10,000 to make a million would he?
Of course he would.
Would you?
Now I've gotten you comfortable with it.
And then the third time, I will tie it down somewhere.
I said, I don't know, some of you think, you know, Jason, if only I had money, then I could make money, right?
And I go, if that was true, then I'd still be broke.
It's not the money that matters.

Are you a resourceful person?
Are you somebody waiting for somebody to hand you an inheritance?

Everyone's like, no, I'm a resourceful person, Jason.

Like that's what he has to be at that level, like watching him to study it.

Like hope you guys all watch it.

I made a goal Russell to once a day for six months straight, memorize a close.

I would just take his book, Secrets of Closing the Cell, write it on a three by five card.
It had to fit on a three by five card.
And the morning I'd write it out, I'd carry it with me, by the end of the night I'd have to close memorized.
And I did that for six months straight.
And I never had to do it ever again.

Most people would say yes, if they were just presented with a situation where they felt shy.
You could ask those same people an hour later if they feel brave.
And if they just did something that they felt was brave, a minute before that, they would say yes, the same person in different circumstances is simultaneously brave and shy.
So we have to make them in that state where they understand that they're able to do that.

We did not talk about the better than Money Back Guarantee Russell.
Oh my God, good thing we're doing this.
I forgot about this, right?
Risk reversal.
The most money I've ever made on webinars has came from asking this question.
Even if they got their money back, what did it cost them to be part of this in time and maybe even expenses?
So like, we saw this as an affiliate, we're the number one affiliate for the largest Facebook marketing course at the time, Facebook advertising.
We went back when it first started and this was the offer I made the audience.
I said, listen, you could buy this course and if it doesn't work for you, get your money back but I don't think that's good enough because if you follow this course, you're gonna spend money on ads.
The real risk is not your investment in the course.
We can agree with that, right?
Yes, of course, Jason.
Now that you've told me we can't agree to it, I will, right?
I said, so here's the deal.
Your real risk here is spending the money on ads.
I am so confident in this product that if you follow it to a T that if you don't make at least three X your investment back on your ads in this period of time, I will personally pay and reimburse you three times what you invested.
So that way, as long as you follow it and put the work in, worst case scenario, you make three times your investment today.
And then anything you make above and beyond that, even better, right?
But in a situation like this, doesn't it make sense for you to risk only making $3,000 in the next 60 days by signing up right now?
And that's a great example of taking the risk beyond just the money back guarantee.
We're literally, I could say, I'm paying for your time because you're gonna go through this course.
Your time is valuable.
Let me pay for it.
Now you have to put conditions in place.
You have to say, do X, do Y, and do C.
The easiest way, by the way, here's the thing.
Russell, I don't know about you, but most of my wins have came because I've consistently made progress day in and day out, right?
I have disciplined myself to follow something through more than just one time here and there.
And so I try to do that with my customers.
And so this is, we typically will loop a better than money back guarantee to making them simply document three things that they did that day for 60 days straight or 45 days straight.
And it's sad, but most people can't do that.
They don't have the discipline just to do one to three small things a day to move forward.
They either want it to happen right away or if it doesn't happen right away, they never do it.
So I know if I can get people to consistently move forward and focus on progress, not on outcome, they're probably gonna get the best outcome.
And of course, if they don't, I'm happy to pay for some double your money back guarantees if it quadruples my conversion rate, right?
It's a math equation.
It's like, it's an investment.
When I spend an extra $15,000, it means an extra $150,000 every day of the week.
So one of the strongest leverage points you can do is if everybody in your market is doing standard guarantees, find a conditional better than money back guarantee.
So the easiest way to start is double your money back.
Listen, this course is 997.
And if you take this and it doesn't work for you, as long as you're willing to follow X, Y and Z, not only will I give you your money back, I'll give you an extra $1,000 for your time, right?
And now that's a double your money back guarantee.
And you could do that with almost anything, right?
One of my favorite ways to do this too, Russell, is we haven't done this in years, but to link it to a charity.
Not only will I give you your money back, but I'll equal that in charitable donation to the charity of your choice.
And now it's like, oh my God, this guy's serious about selling something, right?

So most people just say, here's the vehicle.
They don't have those other two steps, the internal fears and the external ones.

Then I get a bunch of guesses.

And now we have two choices of how we can move forward today.
Choice number one is we can part ways and then hopefully if I saw you in the future, you used this, you applied it and you were successful.

Our option number two is I can play an active role in helping you be successful by giving you everything I have in my willpower and helping and taking personal responsibilities in your outcome by providing you resources, insights, secrets and community to make a go at it with blank.
And if you're interested in the second option, then you're gonna love what I have for you today.

That little subtle shift there has really helped more people I know of get comfortable selling on a webinar than anything else I've ever taught.
